title: Working Tree, Index, Commits, and Atomic History · Repository Checkpoint
summary: Turn a mixed set of feature, formatting, and accidental changes into reviewable commits without losing work. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an atomic commit construction workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.
Read branch, staged, unstaged, untracked, rename, conflict, and ahead/behind status without treating short output as context-free.
Select working-tree, staged, commit-to-commit, path-limited, word, and summary diffs from the review question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Three Tree Model · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff. Competency probe: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.

Which action demonstrates Three Tree Model without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace head, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Atomic Commit Message: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff. Competency probe: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.
steps.why: Retrieving Atomic Commit Message through the concrete an atomic commit construction workbook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.
